title: Feature request: add optional `storage.public-endpoint` for S3 presigned download URLs
author: aHilsberg
body:
Attic currently appears to use the same S3 storage.endpoint both for internal object storage access and for generating presigned download URLs returned to clients.

This is inconvenient when running Attic and S3-compatible storage inside Kubernetes.

In my setup:
- atticd runs inside a Kubernetes cluster
- S3-compatible storage also runs inside the same cluster
- Attic should upload/write data to S3 via the cluster-internal service endpoint
- External Nix clients should download NARs via a public S3 endpoint

Today, if I configure Attic with the public S3 endpoint so that clients receive usable presigned URLs, Attic itself also uses that public endpoint from inside the pod. That means upload/write traffic may hairpin through external DNS and the gateway/load balancer in front of the S3 service, even though the storage is available directly inside the cluster.

It would be useful to have an optional config value such as:
```toml
[storage]
type = "s3"
endpoint = "http://s3.storage.svc.cluster.local:9000"
public-endpoint = "https://s3.example.com"
bucket = "attic"
```

Expected behavior:
- storage.endpoint is used by Attic internally for S3 reads/writes/uploads/deletes
- storage.public-endpoint, when set, is used only when generating presigned URLs returned to clients; if storage.public-endpoint is unset, current behavior is preserved

title: `attic cache configure` unexpectedly requires configure-cache-retention permission
author: aHilsberg
body:
When running attic cache configure, the CLI appears to always include retention_period = Global in the PATCH request, even when no retention-related flag is passed.

This makes normal cache configuration fail unless the token also has configure-cache-retention / cq.

Example:
```bash
atticadm make-token \
  --sub admin \
  --pull '*' \
  --push '*' \
  --create-cache '*' \
  --configure-cache '*' \
  --destroy-cache '*'
```

```bash
attic cache configure my-cache --public
```
Expected: succeeds, because `--public` should only require configure-cache permission.

Actual: fails with:
```
AccessError: User does not have permission to complete this action.
```
A raw PATCH that omits retention_period works with the same token:
```bash
curl -X PATCH "$ENDPOINT/_api/v1/cache-config/my-cache" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{"public":true}'
```

Suggested fix: only send retention_period when `--retention-period` or `--reset-retention-period` is explicitly passed.

I believe the problem to be [this piece](https://github.com/zhaofengli/attic/blob/7a19204df10d606c5070e6bb72615c3461900c05/client/src/command/cache.rs#L224-L229) in case it wasn't intentional.

title: Request feature: upstream cache like nixpkgs or cachix
author: Abdillah
body:
I would like to see, attic has upstream caches, whether it is cachix or official cache.nixos.org.

If the NAR already exists in those cache, it would be skipped. If the cache is requested, user can configure whether to download from the upstream and pass it forward or make it 404.

title: Add user and group in nix modules
author: waynevanson

title: Feature Proposal: support oauth2 accessTokens (in JWT format) for authentication.
author: ToxicMushroom
body: **Goal** - This way users can obtain their own attic tokens configured for their groups/roles. - Less fragmented authentication and access control in multi-service environments. **Required changes (blue in diagram)** For this to work attic would need to trust the oauth2 provider's public keyset. 0. This should be possible via fetching the key endpoint at startup, thus adding 1 config option (e.g. `oauth2_keyset_endpoint`). 2. The oauth2 accessToken (JWT format) would contain the current attic-JWT claims inside. Such that after validating the token, attic can continue as it does today. I expect the change for incoming requests would be rather simple. 3. A conditional parse step when an oauth2 token is detected. 4. The JWT signature validation would conditionally use the public keyset of the oauth2 provider. [attic_oauth2_jwt_signing.drawio](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27394634/attic_oauth2_jwt_signing.drawio) If such a feature is desired upstream then I can create a PR for this :)

title: atticd: Allow reading JWT secrets from named file
author: sepointon
body:
Presently, atticd's JWT secrets can be passed either in the config file or as env vars. systemd credentials are a nice way of plumbing secrets through system containers, but they very strongly want to be passed as files to be read from, rather than as env vars or spliced in to config files.

It's possible to hack around this with shell, but it's ugly:

```
[Service]
ExecStart=${pkgs.bash}/bin/bash -c 'ATTIC_SERVER_TOKEN_RS256_SECRET_BASE64="$(< $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY/attic-token-secret)" ${pkgs.attic-server}/bin/atticd --config ${atticd-config}'
LoadCredential=attic-token-secret
```

compared to an imagined result:

```
[Service]
ExecStart=${pkgs.attic-server}/bin/atticd --config ${atticd-config} --token-rs256-secret-file=$CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY/attic-token-secret
LoadCredential=attic-token-secret
```

title: nixos-disk-image hash mismatch for hwdb.bin
author: johnhamelink
body:
I have managed to run into this issue twice so far while using attic:

```
nixos-disk-image> error: hash mismatch importing path '/nix/store/1yj89iaz6asmgq9raf38mmasizrj5j4w-hwdb.bin';

nixos-disk-image>          specified: sha256:00z4hv4dh0cb64kfg1ffnsrnas6nbvc1vyq29ki86abvwzjifgvv

nixos-disk-image>          got:       sha256:143244vq7wqlh8ywacpgc5b8z4q0pbvd7zpjq6qa6c593fqjsrpp

nixos-disk-image> error: path '/nix/store/1yj89iaz6asmgq9raf38mmasizrj5j4w-hwdb.bin' is required, but there is no substituter that can build it
```

I haven't been able to find a pattern yet, nor a solution that doesn't involve deleting the entire cache & database.

Any advice would be greatly received!

title: `attic use` writes upstream server address instead of reverse proxy address to nix.conf
author: dmitry-j-mikhin
body:
When Attic server runs behind a reverse proxy, `attic use <cache>` writes the **internal upstream address** (`attic_ip:attic_port`) into `~/.config/nix/nix.conf` instead of the **reverse proxy address** (`proxy_ip:proxy_port`) that was originally configured via `attic login`.

This makes the substituter unusable since the upstream server is not directly accessible from the client.

## Setup

```
                        ┌──────────────────────┐
                        │   Attic Server       │
  ┌───────────┐         │  (internal network)  │
  │  Client   │───X────▶│  attic_ip:attic_port │   ← not directly reachable
  └───────────┘         └──────────────────────┘
       │                         ▲
       │                         │ proxy_pass
       │                ┌────────┴─────────────┐
       └───────────────▶│   Reverse Proxy      │
              ✓         │  proxy_ip:proxy_port │   ← reachable
                        └──────────────────────┘
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Deploy Attic server on `attic_ip:attic_port` (not exposed externally).
2. Set up a reverse proxy on `proxy_ip:proxy_port` forwarding to the Attic server.
3. Log in via the proxy address:
   ```bash
   attic login --set-default <name> http://proxy_ip:proxy_port <TOKEN>
   ```
   ✅ This works correctly.
4. Configure the cache as a substituter:
   ```bash
   attic use <cache_name>
   ```
5. Inspect `~/.config/nix/nix.conf`.

## Expected Behavior

```
substituters = http://proxy_ip:proxy_port/<cache_name>
```

The substituter URL should use the **proxy address** — the same endpoint that was provided during `attic login`.

## Actual Behavior

```
substituters = http://attic_ip:attic_port/<cache_name>
```

The substituter URL uses the **internal upstream address**, which is unreachable from the client. The user has to manually edit `nix.conf` every time `attic use` is invoked.

## Root Cause (Hypothesis)

It seems like the Attic server returns its own bind address (or some internally-resolved address) in the API response for the cache endpoint, and `attic use` trusts that value instead of constructing the substituter URL from the **endpoint configured in the client** (`attic login`).

## Workaround

Manually replace the address in `~/.config/nix/nix.conf` after every `attic use` call:

```diff
- substituters = http://attic_ip:attic_port/<cache_name>
+ substituters = http://proxy_ip:proxy_port/<cache_name>
```

## Environment

- **Attic version:** attic-client 0.1.0
- **Reverse proxy:** MITM proxy
- **OS:** Fedora

title: How to use it as a proxy cache server ?
author: drupol
body:
Hello,

I am currently looking into hosting an internal Nix binary cache at home and evaluating the different open-source solutions available.

Attic seems to be exactly the kind of solution I am looking for, so thank you for your work on it !!!

I was wondering whether Attic can also be used as a proxy cache for `cache.nixos.org`.

If so, would you mind sharing the minimal configuration required to set this up?

Thank you very much in advance.

title: watch-store does not work with chroot stores
author: sepointon

title: attic watch-store requires running as trusted user?
author: wereHamster
body: I have nixos machine that's acting as a remote builder. I want all new derivations built on it to be automatically pushed to my cache. The `watch-store` command is perfect for that. I used https://github.com/nix-community/buildbot-nix/blob/main/examples/attic-watch-store.nix as inspiration how to run that command as a systemd service. However, I noticed it only works when the `watch-store` command runs as a trusted user. Is that expected? I couldn't find anything in the documentation that mentions that. The example uses systemd DynamicUser option, and copilot is claiming that my code (see below screenshot) is not correct. I can fix my systemd service definition (for example by defining a fixed user and not using DynamicUser), however I first want to have clarity on the requirements of the `watch-store` command.

title: Google Cloud Storage native support
author: omarjatoi
body:
Hi! I was wondering if attic is open to adding a new [`StorageBackend`](https://github.com/zhaofengli/attic/blob/main/server/src/storage/mod.rs#L46) to support [Google Cloud Storage](https://cloud.google.com/storage) natively. Attic can currently be used with GCS using the [S3 interoperability layer](https://docs.cloud.google.com/storage/docs/interoperability), however this requires using [HMAC keys](https://docs.cloud.google.com/storage/docs/authentication/hmackeys).

Using the official [`google-cloud-storage`](https://crates.io/crates/google-cloud-storage) and [`google-cloud-auth`](https://crates.io/crates/google-cloud-auth) crates we can add a new backend for GCS and use [Application Default Credentials](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/application-default-credentials) so Service Accounts can be used to authenticate with the GCS bucket, which is easier to manage and deploy and preferred for production environments in GCP.

I have a branch with an implementation, see https://github.com/omarjatoi/attic/commit/c33b1a58ab45e129dbdb4d55848d1aa0768aa2f0. If this issue gets a go-ahead I'd be happy to open a PR for review.

Thanks!

title: [FEATURE] Configure hostname based requests for S3
author: zachspar
body:
Right now, `s3.rs` is hard-coding path style requests with the S3 client. Not all storage providers accept this, and we should make it configurable in the `toml` file.

```
2026-02-09T22:34:50.663581Z ERROR attic_server::error: Storage error: Storage error: service error: unhandled error (PathStyleRequestNotAllowed): Error { code: "PathStyleRequestNotAllowed", message: "The path style requests are not allowed for this method, please switch to hostname-based requests.", aws_request_id: "fae25141-6bd3-91d8-bb0e-ca4171f348af" }
```

title: No supported server‑only build; client build hard‑fails on Debian due to Nix dependency
author: julienbardi
body:
**Description**
I’m deploying Attic as a server-only installation on Debian Bookworm and ran into a build-system limitation that makes this use case unnecessarily difficult.

**Environment**
- OS: Debian 12 (Bookworm)
- Hardware: Ugreen DXP 4800+ NAS (x86_64)
- Use case: Attic server-only (atticd) deployment
- Nix version available in Debian: 2.8
- Attic revision: 12cbeca

**What works**
- atticd (the server) builds and runs correctly on Debian Bookworm.
- The server does not depend on Nix or pkg-config at runtime.
- Once built, the service runs fine and accepts connections from external clients.

**What fails**
- The build process unconditionally attempts to build the client (attic).
- The client requires `nix-main >= 2.24` via `pkg-config`.
- Debian Bookworm ships Nix 2.8, with no supported upgrade path.
- As a result, the client build hard-fails on Debian.
There is an environment variable (`NIX_MAIN_NO_PKG_CONFIG`) intended to disable Nix probing, but setting it causes the build to abort rather than skipping the client build. There is currently no supported or documented way to perform a **server-only build.**

**Impact**
This makes it appear that Attic cannot be built on Debian, even though the server component works perfectly. Users deploying Attic as a network service on non-NixOS systems must manually patch the build scripts to remove the client build step.

**Suggested improvements**
Any of the following would resolve the issue cleanly:
- Document that the client cannot be built on Debian and that server-only deployments are supported.
- Provide a supported server-only build mode (e.g. --server-only or ATTIC_BUILD_CLIENT=0).
- Change NIX_MAIN_NO_PKG_CONFIG to skip the client build instead of aborting.
- Make the client build optional rather than unconditional.

<details>

<summary>Failing client bootstrap script (Debian)</summary>

```sh
# Attic client installation (Cargo-based, no Nix)
# fails 
# The system library `nix-main` required by crate `attic` was not found.
# The file `nix-main.pc` needs to be installed
#
ATTIC_REPO        := https://github.com/zhaofengli/attic.git
ATTIC_SRC         := /usr/local/src/attic
ATTIC_CLIENT_BIN  := /usr/local/bin/attic

STAMP_ATTIC_CLIENT := $(STAMP_DIR)/attic-client.installed

.PHONY: install-attic-client remove-attic-client

install-attic-client: ensure-run-as-root
	@echo "📦 Installing Attic client (Cargo build)"
	@$(run_as_root) install -d -m 0755 $(STAMP_DIR)
	@if [ -x "$(ATTIC_CLIENT_BIN)" ] && [ -f "$(STAMP_ATTIC_CLIENT)" ]; then \
		echo "[make] Attic client already installed; skipping"; \
		exit 0; \
	fi
	@if [ ! -d "$(ATTIC_SRC)/.git" ]; then \
		echo "[make] Cloning Attic source"; \
		$(run_as_root) mkdir -p "$(dir $(ATTIC_SRC))"; \
		$(run_as_root) git clone "$(ATTIC_REPO)" "$(ATTIC_SRC)"; \
	fi
	@echo "[make] Checking out Attic revision $(ATTIC_REF)"
	@$(run_as_root) git -C "$(ATTIC_SRC)" \
		-c safe.directory="$(ATTIC_SRC)" \
		fetch --tags
	@$(run_as_root) git -C "$(ATTIC_SRC)" \
		-c safe.directory="$(ATTIC_SRC)" \
		-c advice.detachedHead=false \
		checkout "$(ATTIC_REF)"
	@echo "[make] Building Attic client"
	@$(run_as_root) bash -c 'cd "$(ATTIC_SRC)" && cargo build --release -p attic-client'
	@$(run_as_root) install -m 0755 "$(ATTIC_SRC)/target/release/attic" "$(ATTIC_CLIENT_BIN)"
	@echo "version=$(ATTIC_REF) installed_at=$$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
		| $(run_as_root) tee "$(STAMP_ATTIC_CLIENT)" >/dev/null
	@echo "✅ Attic client installed"

remove-attic-client: ensure-run-as-root
	@echo "🗑️ Removing Attic client"
	@$(run_as_root) rm -f "$(ATTIC_CLIENT_BIN)" "$(STAMP_ATTIC_CLIENT)"
	@echo "✅ Attic client removed"
```
</details>

<details>

<summary>bin/run-as-root.sh (used above)</summary>

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# bin/run-as-root
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# CONTRACT:
# - Accepts argv tokens, not a single quoted string.
# - Preserves argument boundaries exactly.
# - If already root: exec "$@".
# - If not root: exec sudo -- "$@".
# - This allows commands with arguments, quotes, >, |, &&, ||.
# - In Makefiles, escape operators (\>, \|, \&\&) so they survive parsing.
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
set -euo pipefail
[ "$#" -gt 0 ] || {
    echo "run-as-root: no command specified" >&2
    exit 64
}
# Environment variables allowed to cross the privilege boundary.
# This list is policy: changes must be deliberate and reviewed.
PRESERVE_ENV="DEBIAN_FRONTEND,SRC_ATTIC_CONFIG,SRC_ATTIC_SERVICE,ATTIC_REF"
if [ "$(id -u)" -eq 0 ]; then
	exec "$@"
else
	exec sudo --preserve-env="$PRESERVE_ENV" -- "$@"
fi
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>FYI (not relevant for the present issue): Working server-only bootstrap script (Debian)</summary>

```sh
#!/bin/sh
#bootstrap-attic.sh
# Bootstrap Attic server: build, install, configure, and register systemd unit.
# Intended to be invoked by Make as root.
set -eu

ATTIC_ROOT="${ATTIC_ROOT:-/volume1/homelab/attic}"

: "${SRC_ATTIC_CONFIG:?SRC_ATTIC_CONFIG not set}"
: "${SRC_ATTIC_SERVICE:?SRC_ATTIC_SERVICE not set}"
: "${ATTIC_REF:?ATTIC_REF not set (e.g. v0.1.0 or commit hash)}"

DST_ATTIC_CONFIG="${ATTIC_ROOT}/config.toml"
DST_ATTIC_SERVICE="/etc/systemd/system/attic.service"

ATTIC_REPO="https://github.com/zhaofengli/attic.git"
ATTIC_SRC="/usr/local/src/attic"
ATTIC_SERVER_BIN="/usr/local/bin/atticd"

SECRET_FILE="/etc/attic/jwt-hs256-secret"

if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
	echo "ERROR: This script must be run as root"
	exit 1
fi

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Verify required tools
# ------------------------------------------------------------
for tool in openssl git cargo systemctl; do
	if ! command -v "$tool" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
		echo "   • ERROR: Required tool '$tool' not found"
		exit 1
	fi
done

if [ ! -f "$SECRET_FILE" ]; then
	echo "   • Generating Attic JWT secret"
	install -d -o root -g root -m 0700 /etc/attic
	openssl rand -base64 32 > "$SECRET_FILE"
	chmod 0600 "$SECRET_FILE"
fi

echo "→ Bootstrapping Attic"

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Ensure Attic runtime directories exist
# ------------------------------------------------------------
echo "   • Ensuring Attic runtime directories exist"
install -d -o root -g root -m 0755 \
	"${ATTIC_ROOT}" \
	"${ATTIC_ROOT}/logs" \
	"${ATTIC_ROOT}/store" \
	"${ATTIC_ROOT}/index"

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Ensure Attic source checkout exists
# ------------------------------------------------------------
echo "   • Ensuring Attic source checkout"

if [ ! -d "${ATTIC_SRC}/.git" ]; then
	echo "   • Cloning Attic source"
	mkdir -p "$(dirname "${ATTIC_SRC}")"
	git clone "${ATTIC_REPO}" "${ATTIC_SRC}"
fi

echo "   • Checking out Attic revision ${ATTIC_REF}"
cd "${ATTIC_SRC}"
git fetch --tags
git -c advice.detachedHead=false checkout "${ATTIC_REF}"

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Build and install atticd
# ------------------------------------------------------------
echo "   • Building atticd from source"

cd "${ATTIC_SRC}"
git rev-parse --short HEAD >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
	echo "   • ERROR: Unable to read Attic git revision in ${ATTIC_SRC}"
	exit 1
}
git rev-parse --short HEAD | sed 's/^/   • Using Attic revision: /'

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Verify Rust toolchain availability
# ------------------------------------------------------------
if ! command -v cargo >/dev/null 2>&1; then
	echo "   • ERROR: Rust toolchain (cargo) not found"
	echo "     Install Rust before running this bootstrap"
	exit 1
fi

# Build only the Attic server binary.
# The client is intentionally excluded: it is not required to run the server
# and currently pulls in a Nix-based build dependency that breaks on Debian.
cargo build --release -p attic-server

if [ ! -x "target/release/atticd" ]; then
	echo "   • ERROR: atticd build failed"
	exit 1
fi

echo "   • Installing atticd"
install -o root -g root -m 0755 "target/release/atticd" "${ATTIC_SERVER_BIN}.new"
mv -f "${ATTIC_SERVER_BIN}.new" "${ATTIC_SERVER_BIN}"

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Deploy configuration
# ------------------------------------------------------------
if [ -f "${SRC_ATTIC_CONFIG}" ]; then
	echo "   • Deploying config.toml"
	install -o root -g root -m 0644 "${SRC_ATTIC_CONFIG}" "${DST_ATTIC_CONFIG}"
else
	echo "   • WARNING: config.toml missing in repo"
fi
SECRET="$(cat "$SECRET_FILE")"

if [ -f "$DST_ATTIC_CONFIG" ] && \
   ! grep -q '^token-hs256-secret-base64' "$DST_ATTIC_CONFIG"; then
	echo "   • Injecting Attic JWT signing configuration"
	cat >> "$DST_ATTIC_CONFIG" <<EOF

# Injected at install time — do not commit
[jwt.signing]
token-hs256-secret-base64 = "$SECRET"
EOF
fi

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Install systemd service
# ------------------------------------------------------------
if [ -f "${SRC_ATTIC_SERVICE}" ]; then
	echo "   • Installing systemd service"
	install -D -m 0644 "${SRC_ATTIC_SERVICE}" "${DST_ATTIC_SERVICE}"
	systemctl daemon-reload
	systemctl is-enabled attic >/dev/null 2>&1 || systemctl enable attic
else
	echo "   • WARNING: attic.service missing in repo"
fi

echo "→ Attic bootstrap complete"
```
</details>

title: Cache size statistics
author: lheckemann
body:
Some statistics would be helpful.

I've recently set up an attic instance and wanted to evaluate how the storage use compares to a flat-file binary cache, and came up with the following queries:

```
atticd=# select pg_size_pretty(total_nar_size) total_nar_size, pg_size_pretty(total_compressed_nar_size) total_compressed_nar_size, pg_size_pretty(total_chunk_size) uncompressed_chunk_size, pg_size_pretty(total_storage_used) total_storage_used, total_compressed_nar_size / total_storage_used ratio from (select (select sum(nar_size) from nar) total_nar_size, (select sum(chunk_size) from chunk)  total_chunk_size, (select sum(file_size) from chunk) total_storage_used, (select sum(compressed_nar_size) total_compressed_nar_size from (select sum(file_size) compressed_nar_size from nar, chunkref, chunk where nar_id = nar.id and chunk_id = chunk.id group by nar.id)));
 total_nar_size | total_compressed_nar_size | uncompressed_chunk_size | total_storage_used |       ratio        
----------------+---------------------------+-------------------------+--------------------+--------------------
 9157 MB        | 3145 MB                   | 4843 MB                 | 1708 MB            | 1.8419919245644854
(1 row)
```

`ratio` is an approximation of how much space is saved (for nars only, not narinfos!) compared to a flat-file binary cache.

This is a single-tenant instance, so I haven't spent much thought on getting these stats per cache, but they're probably useful as-is even from a multi-tenant operator perspective (though they don't shed any light on how much deduplication happens across tenants). It would probably also not be complicated to get these numbers per cache, which could be useful from an individual tenant perspective as well.

Maybe something like this could be surfaced via the API? Somewhat related to #7.

title: cannot set cache directory under /home
author: Redhawk18

title: Cache Everything Option
author: greg-hellings
body:
Currently Attic will not push anything to its cache that is already present in `cache.nixos.org`. It might check other repos, but I'm not 100% certain on that.

I would like an option to cache EVERYTHING. This would allow use of Attic for caching things on a slow connection, or just not repeating downloads over the Internet. It would allow Attic to serve as a repository when a connection is down or cache.nixos.org is otherwise unreachable. It would allow using Attic as a hermetically sealed box that could serve up everything a user could ever want for an air gapped install process.

title: Trouble setting up atticd
author: Redhawk18
body:
Hi, I've followed the tutorial and I can't get my localhost cache to login, I tried to use the nixos module first and that didn't really work correctly. So I used the shell, but that previous module install is still using the information/tokens the previous install wanted to use. I couldn't find online where attic stores data for servers either.

```
redhawk in 🌐 PopTart in ~ 
❯ attic login local http://localhost:8080 eyJ...
✍ Overwriting server "local"

redhawk in 🌐 PopTart in ~ 
❯ attic cache create hello
Error: error sending request for url (http://localhost:47715/_api/v1/cache-config/hello)

Caused by:
    0: client error (Connect)
    1: tcp connect error
    2: Connection refused (os error 111)
```
